# SimpleSign

> Pure .NET library for PAdES (PDF Advanced Electronic Signatures) — sign, validate, and inspect digitally signed PDF documents. No third-party crypto dependencies. Native AOT compatible. Targets .NET 8 and .NET 10.

SimpleSign implements ETSI EN 319 142 (PAdES) with conformance levels B-B, B-T, B-LT, and B-LTA. All cryptography uses `System.Security.Cryptography` from the BCL — no BouncyCastle, no native libraries.

## Core API

```csharp
// Sign a PDF
var signed = await SimpleSigner.Document(pdfBytes)
    .WithCertificate(cert)
    .WithTimestamp("http://timestamp.digicert.com")
    .WithLtv()
    .SignAsync();

// Validate signatures
var results = await new PdfSignatureValidator(new ValidationOptions
{
    CheckRevocation = true,
    TrustSystemRoots = true
}).ValidateAsync(stream);

// Inspect (fast, no crypto)
var info = await PdfSignatureInspector.InspectAsync(stream);

// Deferred signing (hash on server, sign on client)
var prepared = await DeferredSigner.PrepareAsync(pdfBytes, cert);
byte[] signedPdf = await DeferredSigner.CompleteAsync(prepared.SessionData, clientSignature);

// Batch signing
var batch = BatchSigner.Create(cert).WithTimestamp(url).WithLtv().Build();
var results = await batch.SignAsync(documents);
```

## Packages

```
dotnet add package SimpleSign.PAdES   # PDF signing & validation (most common)
dotnet add package SimpleSign.Brasil  # ICP-Brasil, Gov.br, Lei 14.063
dotnet add package SimpleSign         # Meta-package (all)
```

## Package Map

```
SimpleSign (meta-package)
├── SimpleSign.PAdES     PAdES B-B/T/LT/LTA signing & validation
│   ├── SimpleSign.Pdf   PDF structure parser
│   └── SimpleSign.Core  Crypto primitives, CMS, TSA, revocation
├── SimpleSign.Brasil    ICP-Brasil + Gov.br extensions
└── SimpleSign.HtmlToPdf Pure-.NET HTML→PDF converter
```

## Docs

- [API Reference](docs/api/): Full XML-documented public API
- [Architecture](docs/architecture.md): Internal design
- [Conformance](docs/conformance.md): ETSI standards compliance
- [Interoperability](docs/interoperability.md): Cross-platform testing
- [Performance](docs/performance.md): Benchmarks and optimization

## Key Constraints

- .NET 8+ required (multi-targets net8.0 and net10.0)
- No third-party cryptography (all via System.Security.Cryptography)
- Native AOT compatible
- No native/COM dependencies — runs on Windows, macOS, Linux
- MIT licensed

## Optional

- [Samples](samples/): Working examples for common scenarios
